Name: Casa Lac
Tips: Spain's oldest continuously operating restaurant (est. 1825). Classic Aragonese cuisine in a beautiful historic room. Ternasco al horno (roast milk lamb) and migas aragonesas. Book ahead — tables fill even on weeknights.

Name: El Chalet
Address: C/ de Santa Teresa de Jesús 25 Zaragoza
Tips: One of Zaragoza's best restaurants for contemporary cooking with local Aragonese ingredients. Tasting menu or à la carte seasonal dishes. Book ahead.

Name: La Rinconada de Lorenzo
Tips: Classic Aragonese home cooking. The ternasco asado (roast milk lamb) and Pyrenean trout are outstanding. Walk-in often fine but book to be safe.

Name: El Temple pintxos street
Tips: Zaragoza's answer to Carrer de Blai — a street of pintxos bars. Very local. Go 20:30–22:00. Good warm-up before dinner or light casual meal.

Name: Mercado de las Artes
Address: C/ de San Ignacio de Loyola Zaragoza
Tips: Modern food market in a 1920s space. Multiple stands — good option for a lighter meal or families where everyone wants something different.

Name: Bar El Fútbol
Tips: Classic Aragonese bar breakfast — tostada con tomate, coffee, atmosphere. A local institution; humble, authentic.

Name: Pastelería Fantoba
Tips: Zaragoza's most famous pastry shop since 1856. Trenza de Almudévar (braided almond pastry) is the city's signature sweet. Buy one to eat now and one to take on the train to Madrid.

Name: Bar Gallizo
Tips: Excellent tapas recommended by Airbnb host. Casual neighborhood favourite — 5 min walk from Calle Dr. Alejandro Palomar.

Name: Restaurante El Fuelle
Tips: Traditional Aragonese cooking recommended by Airbnb host. Reserve by phone 976 39 80 33. Classic local spot — close to the apartment.

Name: El Ángel del Pincho
Tips: Pajaritos (small fried birds) and tempura — pintxos-style. Host recommendation. Right next to Bar La Viña for an easy two-stop crawl.

Name: Bar La Viña
Tips: Berenjenas con miel (eggplant with honey) is the signature dish. Host recommendation. Adjacent to El Ángel del Pincho.

Name: The Lynx
Tips: Local atmospheric bar where Guardia Civil hangs out. Host pick — very neighbourhood, no tourists.
